#### **Infant markers**
[Go back to index](#top)

Summary statistics

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_reshaped <- DCHS_biomarker_infant %>% gather(Marker, Levels, 48:65)

# Calculate summary statistics for each marker and group
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_reshaped %>%
    group_by(maternal_hiv, Marker) %>%
    summarise(
    Mean = mean(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    SD = sd(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Median = median(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    IQR = IQR(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Pivot the data to have separate columns for each maternal_hiv
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table_wide <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = maternal_hiv, values_from = c("Mean", "SD", "Median", "IQR"))
```

<br>

*t*-test for normally-distributed data

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Ttest_analysis <- map_df(names(DCHS_biomarker_infant_scaled)[48:65], function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste(x, '~ maternal_hiv'))
    t_test(data = DCHS_biomarker_infant_scaled, formula = f, p.adjust.method = "BH", detailed = TRUE) %>%
      mutate(Marker = x)
  })
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Ttest_analysis$p.adj <- p.adjust(DCHS_biomarker_infant_Ttest_analysis$p, 
                                                           method = "BH") # Calculate adjusted p-values
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Ttest_analysis <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_Ttest_analysis %>%
    select(Marker, group1, n1, group2, n2, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  
# Filter according to final normality results, run t-test only on normally-distributed marker levels
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table_wide_ttest <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table_wide %>%
    filter(!Marker %in% shapiro_infant_results)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table_wide_ttest,
                                                 DCHS_biomarker_infant_Ttest_analysis, 
                                                 by = "Marker")
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(p, p.adj), ~ round(.x, 2))) # Round decimals
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$NegMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$`Median_HIV negative`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$`IQR_HIV negative`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$n1, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$PosMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$`Median_HIV positive`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$`IQR_HIV positive`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$n2, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL %>%
    select(Marker, NegMoms, PosMoms, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  names(DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL) <- c("Marker", "CHU", "CHEU", 
                                                        "Effect size", "95% CI (low)", "95% CI (high)", 
                                                        "P value", "BH-corrected P") # Tidy table 

# Rename markers
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$Marker <- factor(DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$Marker, 
                           levels = c("I6_TNFa",
                                      "I6_CD14",
                                      "I6_CD163",
                                      "I6_YKL40"
                                    ))

  infant_biomarker_rename <- c(
    "I6_TNFa" = "TNFα",
    "I6_CD14" = "CD14",
    "I6_CD163" = "CD163",
    "I6_YKL40" = "YKL-40"
  )
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL <-DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL %>%
    mutate(Marker = recode(Marker, !!!infant_biomarker_rename))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL %>%
    arrange(Marker)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL, "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
      column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$`P value` < 0.05) %>%
      column_spec(column = 8, bold = DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL$`BH-corrected P` < 0.05) 
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_ttest_ALL 
```

<br>

Data is presented as mean ±SD per each group.

*Results*

Prior to BH adjustment, levels of monocyte activation marker **CD14** are **significantly higher in children who are HEU** compared to HU. However, none of these results held after multiple comparison correction.

<br>

Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test (Mann-Whitney U Test) for not normally-distributed data

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_analysis <- map_df(names(DCHS_biomarker_infant_scaled)[48:65], function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste(x, '~ maternal_hiv'))
    wilcox_test(data = DCHS_biomarker_infant_scaled, formula = f, detailed = TRUE) %>%
      mutate(Marker = x)
  })
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_analysis$p.adj <- p.adjust(DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_analysis$p, 
                                                              method = "BH") # Calculate adjusted p-values
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_analysis <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_analysis %>%
    select(Marker, group1, n1, group2, n2, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  
# Filter according to final normality results, run t-test only on normally-distributed marker levels
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table_wide_Wilcoxon <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table_wide %>%
    filter(Marker %in% shapiro_infant_results)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_infant_summary_table_wide_Wilcoxon,
                                                 DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_analysis, 
                                                 by = "Marker")
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(p, p.adj), ~ round(.x, 2))) # Round decimals
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$NegMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$`Median_HIV negative`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$`IQR_HIV negative`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$n1, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$PosMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$`Median_HIV positive`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$`IQR_HIV positive`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$n2, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    select(Marker, NegMoms, PosMoms, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  names(DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL) <- c("Marker", "CHU", "CHEU", 
                                                        "Median difference", "95% CI (low)", "95% CI (high)", 
                                                        "P value", "BH-corrected P") # Tidy table 

# Rename markers
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$Marker <- factor(DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$Marker, 
                           levels = c("I6_GM_CSF",
                                      "I6_IFNg",
                                      "I6_IL1b",
                                      "I6_IL2",
                                      "I6_IL5",
                                      "I6_IL6",
                                      "I6_IL7",
                                      "I6_IL8",
                                      "I6_IL4",
                                      "I6_IL10",
                                      "I6_IL12p70",
                                      "I6_IL13",
                                      "I6_Lcn2",
                                      "I6_MMP9"
                                    ))

infant_biomarker_rename <- c(
  "I6_GM_CSF" = "GM-CSF",
  "I6_IFNg" = "IFNγ",
  "I6_IL1b" = "IL-1β",
  "I6_IL2" = "IL-2",
  "I6_IL5" = "IL-5",
  "I6_IL6" = "IL-6",
  "I6_IL7" = "IL-7",
  "I6_IL8" = "IL-8",
  "I6_IL4" = "IL-4",
  "I6_IL10" = "IL-10",
  "I6_IL12p70" = "IL-12p70",
  "I6_IL13" = "IL-13",
  "I6_Lcn2" = "NGAL",
  "I6_MMP9" = "MMP-9"
)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL <-DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    mutate(Marker = recode(Marker, !!!infant_biomarker_rename))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    arrange(Marker)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL, "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
      column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$`P value` < 0.05) %>%
      column_spec(column = 8, bold = DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL$`BH-corrected P` < 0.05) 
  
  DCHS_biomarker_infant_Wilcoxon_ALL 
```

<br>

Data is presented as median (IQR) per each group.

*Results*

Prior to BH adjustment, levels of pro-inflammatory cytokine **IL-1β** and neuroinflammatory marker **NGAL** are **significantly lower in children who are HEU** compared to HU. However, none of these results held after multiple comparison correction.

<br>
<br>

#### **Child markers at age 2 years**
[Go back to index](#top)

Summary statistics

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_reshaped <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr %>% gather(Marker, Levels, 48:65)

# Calculate summary statistics for each marker and group
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_reshaped %>%
    group_by(maternal_hiv, Marker) %>%
    summarise(
    Mean = mean(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    SD = sd(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Median = median(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    IQR = IQR(Levels, na.rm = TRUE), 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Pivot the data to have separate columns for each maternal_hiv
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table_wide <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = maternal_hiv, values_from = c("Mean", "SD", "Median", "IQR"))
```

<br>

*t*-test for normally-distributed data

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Ttest_analysis <- map_df(names(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_scaled)[48:65], function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste(x, '~ maternal_hiv'))
    t_test(data = DCHS_biomarker_2yr_scaled, formula = f, p.adjust.method = "BH", detailed = TRUE) %>%
      mutate(Marker = x)
  })
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Ttest_analysis$p.adj <- p.adjust(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Ttest_analysis$p, 
                                                           method = "BH") # Calculate adjusted p-values
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Ttest_analysis <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Ttest_analysis %>%
    select(Marker, group1, n1, group2, n2, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  
# Filter according to final normality results, run t-test only on normally-distributed marker levels
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table_wide_ttest <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table_wide %>%
    filter(!Marker %in% shapiro_2yr_results)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table_wide_ttest,
                                                 DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Ttest_analysis, 
                                                 by = "Marker")
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(p, p.adj), ~ round(.x, 2))) # Round decimals
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$NegMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$`Median_HIV negative`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$`IQR_HIV negative`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$n1, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$PosMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$`Median_HIV positive`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$`IQR_HIV positive`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$n2, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL %>%
    select(Marker, NegMoms, PosMoms, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  names(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL) <- c("Marker", "CHU", "CHEU", 
                                            "Effect size", "95% CI (low)", "95% CI (high)", 
                                            "P value", "BH-corrected P") # Tidy table 

# Rename markers
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$Marker <- factor(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$Marker, 
                           levels = c("I24_GM_CSF",
                                      "I24_IFNg",
                                      "I24_IL6",
                                      "I24_IL7",
                                      "I24_TNFa",
                                      "I24_IL10",
                                      "I24_IL13",
                                      "I24_CD14",
                                      "I24_CD163",
                                      "I24_Lcn2"
                                    ))

  child_2yr_biomarker_rename <- c(
    "I24_GM_CSF" = "GM-CSF",
    "I24_IFNg" = "IFNγ",
    "I24_IL6" = "IL-6",
    "I24_IL7" = "IL-7",
    "I24_TNFa" = "TNFα",
    "I24_IL10" = "IL-10",
    "I24_IL13" = "IL-13",
    "I24_CD14" = "CD14",
    "I24_CD163" = "CD163",
    "I24_Lcn2" = "NGAL"
  )
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL <-DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL %>%
    mutate(Marker = recode(Marker, !!!child_2yr_biomarker_rename))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL %>%
    arrange(Marker)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL, "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
      column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$`P value` < 0.05) %>%
      column_spec(column = 8, bold = DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL$`BH-corrected P` < 0.05) 
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_ttest_ALL 
```

<br>

Data is presented as mean ±SD per each group.

*Results*

No significant group differences are detected at age 2 years.

<br>

Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test (Mann-Whitney U Test) for not normally-distributed data

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_analysis <- map_df(names(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_scaled)[48:65], function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste(x, '~ maternal_hiv'))
    wilcox_test(data = DCHS_biomarker_2yr_scaled, formula = f, detailed = TRUE) %>%
      mutate(Marker = x)
  })
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_analysis$p.adj <- p.adjust(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_analysis$p, 
                                                              method = "BH") # Calculate adjusted p-values
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_analysis <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_analysis %>%
    select(Marker, group1, n1, group2, n2, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  
# Filter according to final normality results, run t-test only on normally-distributed marker levels
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table_wide_Wilcoxon <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table_wide %>%
    filter(Marker %in% shapiro_2yr_results)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_summary_table_wide_Wilcoxon,
                                                 DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_analysis, 
                                                 by = "Marker")
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(p, p.adj), ~ round(.x, 2))) # Round decimals
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$NegMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`Median_HIV negative`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`IQR_HIV negative`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$n1, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$PosMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`Median_HIV positive`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`IQR_HIV positive`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$n2, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    select(Marker, NegMoms, PosMoms, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  names(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL) <- c("Marker", "CHU", "CHEU", 
                                                "Median difference", "95% CI (low)", "95% CI (high)", 
                                                "P value", "BH-corrected P") # Tidy table 

# Rename markers
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$Marker <- factor(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$Marker, 
                           levels = c("I24_IL1b",
                                      "I24_IL2",
                                      "I24_IL5",
                                      "I24_IL8",
                                      "I24_IL4",
                                      "I24_IL12p70",
                                      "I24_MMP9",
                                      "I24_YKL40"
                                    ))

child_2yr_biomarker_rename <- c(
  "I24_IL1b" = "IL-1β",
  "I24_IL2" = "IL-2",
  "I24_IL5" = "IL-5",
  "I24_IL8" = "IL-8",
  "I24_IL4" = "IL-4",
  "I24_IL12p70" = "IL-12p70",
  "I24_MMP9" = "MMP-9",
  "I24_YKL40" = "YKL-40"
)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <-DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    mutate(Marker = recode(Marker, !!!child_2yr_biomarker_rename))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    arrange(Marker)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL, "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
      column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`P value` < 0.05) %>%
      column_spec(column = 8, bold = DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`BH-corrected P` < 0.05) 
  
  DCHS_biomarker_2yr_Wilcoxon_ALL 
```

<br>

Data is presented as median (IQR) per each group.

*Results*

Prior to BH adjustment, levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines **IL-1β** and **IL-2**, as well as anti-inflammatory cytokines **IL-4** and **IL-12p70** are **significantly lower in children who are HEU** compared to HU. However, none of these results held after multiple comparison correction.

<br>
<br>
<br>

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_analysis <- map_df(names(DCHS_biomarker_3yr_scaled)[48:65], function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste(x, '~ maternal_hiv'))

  })

                                                              method = "BH") # Calculate adjusted p-values
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_analysis <- DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_analysis %>%
    select(Marker, group1, n1, group2, n2, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  
# Filter according to final normality results, run t-test only on normally-distributed marker levels
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_summary_table_wide_Wilcoxon <- DCHS_biomarker_3yr_summary_table_wide %>%
    filter(Marker %in% shapiro_3yr_results)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_3yr_summary_table_wide_Wilcoxon,
                                                 DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_analysis, 
                                                 by = "Marker")
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(p, p.adj), ~ round(.x, 2))) # Round decimals
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$NegMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`Median_HIV negative`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`IQR_HIV negative`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$n1, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$PosMoms <- 
    paste(DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`Median_HIV positive`,
          "(", DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`IQR_HIV positive`, ")", "(n=",
          DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$n2, ")")

  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    select(Marker, NegMoms, PosMoms, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  names(DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL) <- c("Marker", "CHU", "CHEU", 
                                                "Median difference", "95% CI (low)", "95% CI (high)", 
                                                "P value", "BH-corrected P") # Tidy table 

# Rename markers
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$Marker <- factor(DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$Marker, 
                           levels = c("I36_IFNg",
                                      "I36_IL2",
                                      "I36_IL5",
                                      "I36_IL7",
                                      "I36_IL8",
                                      "I36_IL4",
                                      "I36_IL10",
                                      "I36_IL13",
                                      "I36_CD14",
                                      "I36_YKL40"
                                    ))

child_3yr_biomarker_rename <- c(
  "I36_IFNg" = "IFNγ",
  "I36_IL2" = "IL-2",
  "I36_IL5" = "IL-5",
  "I36_IL7" = "IL-7",
  "I36_IL8" = "IL-8",
  "I36_IL4" = "IL-4",
  "I36_IL10" = "IL-10",
  "I36_IL13" = "IL-13",
  "I36_CD14" = "CD14",
  "I36_YKL40" = "YKL-40"
)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <-DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    mutate(Marker = recode(Marker, !!!child_3yr_biomarker_rename))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL %>%
    arrange(Marker)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL, "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
      column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`P value` < 0.05) %>%
      column_spec(column = 8, bold = DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL$`BH-corrected P` < 0.05) 
  
  DCHS_biomarker_3yr_Wilcoxon_ALL 
```

<br>

Data is presented as median (IQR) per each group.

*Results*

Prior to BH adjustment, levels of monocyte activation marker **CD14** are **significantly higher in children who are HEU** compared to HU. 
However, none of these results held after multiple comparison correction.

*t*-test for normally-distributed data

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_Ttest_analysis <- map_df(names(DCHS_biomarker_5yr_scaled)[48:65], function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste(x, '~ maternal_hiv'))
    t_test(data = DCHS_biomarker_5yr_scaled, formula = f, p.adjust.method = "BH", detailed = TRUE) %>%
      mutate(Marker = x)
  })
  
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_Ttest_analysis$p.adj <- p.adjust(DCHS_biomarker_5yr_Ttest_analysis$p, 
                                                           method = "BH") # Calculate adjusted p-values
  
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_Ttest_analysis <- DCHS_biomarker_5yr_Ttest_analysis %>%
    select(Marker, group1, n1, group2, n2, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p, p.adj)
  
# Filter according to final normality results, run t-test only on normally-distributed marker levels
  
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_summary_table_wide_ttest <- DCHS_biomarker_5yr_summary_table_wide %>%
    filter(!Marker %in% shapiro_5yr_results)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_5yr_summary_table_wide_ttest,
                                                 DCHS_biomarker_5yr_Ttest_analysis,

          DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL$n2, ")")

                                            "Effect size", "95% CI (low)", "95% CI (high)", 
                                            "P value", "BH-corrected P") # Tidy table 

# Rename markers
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL$Marker <- factor(DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL$Marker, 
                           levels = c("I60_GM_CSF",
                                      "I60_IFNg",

                                      "I60_CD14",
                                      "I60_MMP9"
                                    ))

    "I60_IFNg" = "IFNγ",
    "I60_IL4" = "IL-4",
    "I60_CD14" = "CD14",
    "I60_MMP9" = "MMP-9"
  )
  
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL <-DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL %>%
    mutate(Marker = recode(Marker, !!!child_5yr_biomarker_rename))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL <- DCHS_biomarker_5yr_ttest_ALL %>%
```

Data is presented as median (IQR) per each group.

*Results*

Prior to BH adjustment, levels of pro-inflammatory cytokine **IL-5** and anti-inflammatory cytokine **IL-10** are **significantly lower in children who are HEU** compared to HU. Group differences in levels of anti-inflammatory cytokine **IL-10** held after BH correction.

## **Serum marker trajectories** 
[Go back to index](#top)

### **Research question #2**
Are child peripheral blood serum marker trajectories from 6 weeks to 5 years of age altered in children who are HEU, compared to HU?  

### **Hypothesis #2**
Trajectories of serum neuroinflammatory and monocyte activation markers are altered in children who are HEU compared with HU. 

### **Dealing with missing longitudinal data**

When merging serum marker datasets from 6 weeks to 5 years of age via outer join, there are **n=218 children** (**81.96%**, **85 CHEU**, **133 CHU**) with available serum marker data in at least one timepoint, out of the N=268 children invited for neuroimaging at age 6-7 years.

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_6yr_clean <- DCHS_biomarker_6yr[!apply(
  DCHS_biomarker_6yr[, match("M_GM_CSF", names(DCHS_biomarker_6yr)):match("I60_CD14", names(DCHS_biomarker_6yr))], 
  1, function(row) all(is.na(row))
), ]

print(summary(DCHS_biomarker_6yr_clean$maternal_hiv))
```

<br>

At each timepoint, these are the missing values on serum marker levels, grouped by maternal HIV status. The denominator is the N=268 children invited for neuroimaging at age 6-7 years.

```{r}
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled <- DCHS_biomarker_6yr[, -c(5:53)] # Select data
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled[,5:94] <- log(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled[,5:94]) # log-scale data 
  
  names(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled) <- gsub("_(?=CSF)", "", names(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled), perl = TRUE)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled %>% # Reshape data to long format
    pivot_longer(cols = I6_GMCSF:I60_CD14) %>% 
    rename("Levels" = "value", "Marker"= "name")
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long %>% 
    separate(Marker, into = c("Timepoint", "Marker"), sep = "_")
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long$Timepoint <- ordered(
    DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long$Timepoint, levels = c("I6", "I24", "I36", "I60"))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long$Marker <- ordered(
    DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long$Marker, levels = c("GMCSF", # Rename to cute labels
                                                                    "IFNg",
                                                                    "IL1b",
                                                                    "IL2",
                                                                    "IL5",
                                                                    "IL6",
                                                                    "IL7",
                                                                    "IL8",
                                                                    "TNFa",
                                                                    "IL4",
                                                                    "IL10",
                                                                    "IL12p70",
                                                                    "IL13",
                                                                    "CD14",
                                                                    "CD163",
                                                                    "Lcn2",
                                                                    "MMP9",
                                                                    "YKL40"
                                                                  ))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_missing <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long %>%
    group_by(maternal_hiv, Marker, Timepoint) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      n_missing = sum(is.na(Levels)),
      Percent_Missing = round(mean(is.na(Levels)) * 100, 1),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_table_format <- function(n, N, percent) {
    sprintf("%d/%d (%.1f%%)", n, N, percent)
  }
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_missing_table <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_missing %>%
    mutate(Result = DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_table_format(n_missing, N, Percent_Missing)) %>%
    select(maternal_hiv, Marker, Timepoint, Result) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = c(maternal_hiv, Timepoint),
      values_from = Result
    ) %>%
    rename(
      "CHU n/N (%) 6 weeks" = `HIV negative_I6`,
      "CHU n/N (%) 2 years" = `HIV negative_I24`,
      "CHU n/N (%) 3 years" = `HIV negative_I36`,
      "CHU n/N (%) 5 years" = `HIV negative_I60`,
      "CHEU n/N (%) 6 weeks" = `HIV positive_I6`,
      "CHEU n/N (%) 2 years" = `HIV positive_I24`,
      "CHEU n/N (%) 3 years" = `HIV positive_I36`,
      "CHEU n/N (%) 5 years" = `HIV positive_I60`
    ) %>%
    select(Marker, 
           `CHU n/N (%) 6 weeks`, 
           `CHU n/N (%) 2 years`, 
           `CHU n/N (%) 3 years`, 
           `CHU n/N (%) 5 years`,
           `CHEU n/N (%) 6 weeks`, 
           `CHEU n/N (%) 2 years`,
           `CHEU n/N (%) 3 years`, 
           `CHEU n/N (%) 5 years`)
  
  # Display the table
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_missing_kable <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_missing_table %>%
    kable("html", caption = "Percentage (%) of missing data per HIV status and timepoint") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_missing_kable
```

Linear mixed-effects models run using the function `lmer()` from the `lme4` package are ideal in these cases. They handle longitudinal serum marker measurements and account for repeated measures within children. The `lmer()` models can manage missing data at some time points without requiring complete cases or imputation. They include both fixed effects (maternal HIV status, time) and random effects (child-specific intercepts), capturing population-level trends and individual variability. This approach provides accurate estimates and p-values, even with unbalanced designs or missing data, making `lmer()` models powerful for analysing serum marker trajectories and the effects of maternal HIV status over time.

The primary reason for missing data is that some participants did not attend their appointment on one or the other data collection timepoints. Therefore, we would contextually assume data is **missing at random**. However, based on the logistic regression (`glm`) below, data is not missing *completely* at random as both HIV status (p<0.0001) and data collection timepoint (age 3 years p < 0.05; age 5 years p<0.0001) are significant predictors of missingness.

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long$is_missing <- is.na(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long$Levels)
DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_logit <- glm(is_missing ~ maternal_hiv + Timepoint, 
                      data = DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long, family = "binomial")
 
print(summary(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_logit))
```

<br>

### **Linear Mixed-Effects Models** with random intercepts for each subject

Longitudinal changes in serum marker levels were analysed using linear mixed-effects models. The models included fixed effects for timepoint, maternal HIV status, and their interaction, with random intercepts for each subject. Maximum likelihood estimation was used to handle missing data, allowing for the inclusion of all available measurements even when some timepoints were missing. P-values were adjusted for multiple comparisons using the Benjamini-Hochberg method to control the false discovery rate.

This will be run using the function `lmer()` from the `lme4` package:

- Maximum likelihood estimation (`REML = FALSE`) used to handle missing data

- P-values were adjusted for multiple comparisons using the BH method

- ICC represents the proportion of variance explained by between-subject differences, and it is calculated:

$$
ICC = \frac{\text{variance}(\text{random intercept})}{\text{variance}(\text{random intercept}) + \text{residual variance}}
$$

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Define function to fit lmer model
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco <- function(data) {
    lmer(Levels ~ Timepoint * maternal_hiv + (1 | pid_child), 
         data = data,
         REML = FALSE) 
  }                    

# Fit models and extract p-values
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list <- 
    lapply(split(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long, 
                 DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_scaled_long$Marker), 
           DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco)
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list

# Extract p-values and correct for multiple comparisons using BH method
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list_extract_pvals <- function(model_summary) {
    pvals <- coef(summary(model_summary))[, "Pr(>|t|)"]
    names(pvals) <- rownames(coef(summary(model_summary)))
    return(pvals)
  }

  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_pvals <- 
    lapply(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list, 
           DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list_extract_pvals)
  
  all_pvals <- unlist(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_pvals) # Flatten the list of p-values into a single vector
  
  adjusted_pvals <- p.adjust(all_pvals, method = "BH") # Apply BH correction
  
  split_pvals <- split(adjusted_pvals, # Split the adjusted p-values back into the original list structure
                       rep(names(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_pvals), 
                           times = sapply(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_pvals, length)))
  
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_pvals_adjusted <- # Combine the adjusted p-values with the original results
    mapply(function(pvals, adj_pvals) {
      data.frame(
        Term = names(pvals),
        PValue = pvals,
        BH_adjusted_P = adj_pvals
      )
    }, DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_pvals, split_pvals, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
```

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_results <- function(model, marker_name) {
  # Get fixed effects
  fe <- tidy(model, effects = "fixed") %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = sprintf("%.3f", estimate),
      std.error = sprintf("%.3f", std.error),
      statistic = sprintf("%.2f", statistic),
      p.value = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001", 
                      sprintf("%.3f", p.value))
    )
  
  # Get sample size and random effects
  n_obs <- nobs(model)
  n_subjects <- length(unique(model@frame$pid_child))
  re <- VarCorr(model)
  icc <- as.numeric(re$pid_child[1]) / (as.numeric(re$pid_child[1]) + sigma(model)^2)
  
  # Add additional info
  fe$marker <- marker_name
  fe$n_obs <- n_obs
  fe$n_subjects <- n_subjects
  fe$icc <- sprintf("%.3f", icc)
  
  return(fe)
}

DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_results_list <- mapply(DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_results, 
                      DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list,
                      names(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list),
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE)

# Combine results
DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table <- do.call(rbind, DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_results_list)

# Adjust p-values across all markers based on raw p-values from the fixed effects
# We first convert p.values to numeric (handle '<0.001')
raw_p_numeric <- sapply(DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table$p.value, function(x) {
  if(x == "<0.001") return(0.001) else return(as.numeric(x))
})

adj_p <- p.adjust(raw_p_numeric, method = "BH")
DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table$bh_adjusted_p <- ifelse(adj_p < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", adj_p))

# Rearrange columns
DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table[, c("marker", "term", "estimate", "std.error", "statistic", "df", "p.value", "bh_adjusted_p", "n_obs", "n_subjects", "icc")]
```

<br>

**Results**

- Term: Fixed effect parameter

- β: Parameter estimate

- SE: Standard error

- T-value: Test statistic

- DF: Degrees of freedom

- P-value: Unadjusted p-value
    
- BH p-value: Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-value

- N observations: Number of observations. This represents the total number of timepoints included per serum marker in the analysis. It counts every recorded value across all subjects. Due to missing timepoints for some subjects, the total count is lower than the ideal number if every participant had every timepoint available.

- N subjects: Number of unique individuals (using `pid_child`) who contributed at least one observation for a specific serum marker. Since not every subject may have data recorded at every timepoint, this number tells us how many distinct participants' data were incorporated into the model.

- ICC: Intraclass correlation coefficient. When expressed from decimals (in the table) to percentages (%), this metric quantifies the proportion of the total variance attributable to between-subject differences. A higher ICC indicates that most of the variability in the data is due to differences between subjects.

**About the timepoint contrasts**:

These coefficients represent the effect of different aspects (e.g., linear, quadratic, cubic) of time on a specific serum marker levels:

- **Linear contrast** (`Timepoint.L`): Indicates whether there is a linear trend (positive or negative) over time in levels of a specific marker for the reference group (HU children). In other words, it shows if there is an overall increase, or decrease, in marker levels as time progresses in a straight-line manner.

- **Quadratic contrast** (`Timepoint.Q`): Captures curvature in the time trajectory. A negative value, for example, suggests that after the initial increase, the rate of change in levels of a specific serum marker might slow or even reverse at some point.

- **Cubic contrast** (`Timepoint.C`): Further refines the shape of the time trajectory, accounting for more complex, non-linear patterns.

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table %>%
  rename(
    "Marker" = "marker",
    "Term" = "term",
    "β" = "estimate",
    "SE" = "std.error",
    "T-value" = "statistic",
    "DF" = "df",
    "P-value" = "p.value",
    "BH p-value" = "bh_adjusted_p",
    "N observations" = "n_obs",
    "N subjects" = "n_subjects",
    "ICC" = "icc"
  )

LMM_kable_intercept_rows <- which(DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table$Term == "(Intercept)")

DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_kable <- DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table %>%
  kable("html", caption = "Linear Mixed-Effects Models: Results") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(LMM_kable_intercept_rows, background = "lightgray") %>%
  column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table$`P-value` < 0.05) %>%
  column_spec(column = 8, bold = DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_table$`BH p-value` < 0.05) 
DCHS_biomarker_trajectores_all_varco_list_LMM_kable
```

<br>

#### **Model diagnostics**

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.show='hide'}
DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_diagnostics <- function(model, biomarker_name) {

  # Output folder 
  out_dir <- file.path("Exploratory analysis plots", "Linear mixed-effects models", "QC")
  dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  # Extract residuals
  residuals <- resid(model)

  # Residuals vs. Fitted
  plot_resid_fitted <- ggplot(
    data.frame(Fitted = fitted(model), Residuals = residuals),
    aes(Fitted, Residuals)
  ) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(x = "Fitted values", y = "Residuals",
         title = paste("Residuals vs. Fitted for Marker:", biomarker_name))

  # Q-Q plot of residuals
  plot_qq <- ggplot(data.frame(Residuals = residuals), aes(sample = Residuals)) +
    geom_qq() +
    geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = paste("Q-Q Plot of Residuals for Marker:", biomarker_name))

  # Combine & save
  combined_plots <- plot_resid_fitted + plot_qq + plot_layout(ncol = 2)
  plot_filename <- paste0("Diagnostic_Plot_", gsub(" ", "_", biomarker_name), ".png")
  ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, plot_filename),
         plot = combined_plots, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}

DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_plots <- mapply(
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_diagnostics,
  DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list,
  names(DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_all_varco_list)
)
```

```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, results='hide'}
DCHS_biomarker_trajectories_6yr_filtered <- DCHS_biomarker_6yr[, -c(5:53)] # Select data

    iqr = IQR(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower = med - 3 * iqr,

  ungroup()
```

## **Neurometabolite levels** 
[Go back to index](#top)

As per [**Williams *et al* (2023)**](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/neuroscience/articles/10.3389/fnins.2023.1251575/full), LCModel was used to obtain absolute regional metabolite concentrations and relative metabolite ratios to total creatine (Cr+PCr). A supplied *in vitro* generated basis set of individual metabolite spectra was used to quantify metabolite concentrations in LCModel, where absolute metabolite concentrations and metabolite ratios to Cr+PCr ratios were determined. For partial volume correction and water concentration estimation, each voxel was segmented into gray matter, white matter, and cerebrospinal fluid using SPM12 software. Spectra were excluded if they were acquired with a TE greater than 30ms, or if no water reference data were available, and if spectrum distortions, abnormal lipid peaks, or poor spectra baseline were observed on visual inspection. 

In addition, spectra with a full width at half length (FWHM) greater than 0.09 ppm and a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) less than 15 were excluded from our analysis, as well as neurometabolites with Cramér-Rao Lower Bounds (CRLB) greater than 20.

### **Is there a problem with absolute concentrations?**
[Go back to index](#top)

#### **Context of the issue**

The MRS data collection period at 6–7 years of age was affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, during which national lockdown measures implemented by the South African government necessitated a temporary suspension of research activities from early to late 2020. During the present analysis, I identified an unforeseen methodological inconsistency that took place only upon resumption of data acquisition, from late 2020, which led us to discuss a refined approach to data selection and analysis.

#### **Identified irregularities**

**Neurometabolite ratios to total creatine** remained within the expected physiological range for human studies, pre- and post-lockdown

```{r}
DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_rel_diagnostics <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm 

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_rel_diagnostics <- DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_rel_diagnostics %>%
  select(scandate, `Glu/Cr+PCr`, `Ins/Cr+PCr`, `NAA/Cr+PCr`, `GPC+PCh/Cr+PCr`, maternal_hiv, sex) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(scandate, maternal_hiv, sex), names_to = "Neurometabolite", values_to = "Concentration")

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_rel_diagnostics_plot <- ggplot(DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_rel_diagnostics, 
                                                    aes(x = scandate, 
                                                        y = Concentration, 
                                                        color = Neurometabolite)) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.25), size = 4, alpha = 0.4) +
  xlab("Scan date") + ylab("Ratios to creatine") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 16)) +
  theme_classic() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size=16, face="bold"),
                          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold", colour = "black"),
                          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),  
                          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          strip.background = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Parietal grey matter") 

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_rel_diagnostics <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm 

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_rel_diagnostics <- DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_rel_diagnostics %>%
  select(scandate, `Glu/Cr+PCr`, `Ins/Cr+PCr`, `NAA/Cr+PCr`, `GPC+PCh/Cr+PCr`, maternal_hiv, sex) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(scandate, maternal_hiv, sex), names_to = "Neurometabolite", values_to = "Concentration")

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_rel_diagnostics_plot <- ggplot(DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_rel_diagnostics, 
                                                    aes(x = scandate, 
                                                        y = Concentration, 
                                                        color = Neurometabolite)) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.25), size = 4, alpha = 0.4) +
  xlab("Scan date") + ylab("Ratios to creatine") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 16)) +
  theme_classic() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size=16, face="bold"),
                          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold", colour = "black"),
                          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),  
                          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          strip.background = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Parietal white matter") 
```

![](C:/Users/fbert/Dropbox/LABORAL/UCT/Research Article 2/Exploratory analysis plots/MRS data diagnostics/Rel PGM.png){width="100%"}

![](C:/Users/fbert/Dropbox/LABORAL/UCT/Research Article 2/Exploratory analysis plots/MRS data diagnostics/Rel PWM.png){width="100%"}

<br>

However, **absolute neurometabolite concentrations** (more specifically in the white matter voxel) displayed highly anomalous values post-lockdown. In some cases, for example, absolute N-acetyl-aspartate (NAA) levels  exceeded 100 mM, well beyond expected values in human MRS studies.

```{r}
DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_diagnostics <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm 

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_diagnostics <- DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_diagnostics %>%
  select(scandate, Glu, Ins, NAA, `GPC+PCh`, maternal_hiv, sex) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(scandate, maternal_hiv, sex), names_to = "Neurometabolite", values_to = "Concentration")

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_diagnostics_plot <- ggplot(DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_diagnostics, 
                                                    aes(x = scandate, 
                                                        y = Concentration, 
                                                        color = Neurometabolite)) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.5), size = 4, alpha = 0.4) +
  xlab("Scan date") + ylab("Absolute concentrations, mM") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 16)) +
  theme_classic() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size=16, face="bold"),
                          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),
                          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),  
                          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),
                          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
                          strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          strip.background = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Parietal grey matter") 

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm 

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics <- DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics %>%
  select(scandate, Glu, Ins, NAA, `GPC+PCh`, maternal_hiv, sex) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(scandate, maternal_hiv, sex), names_to = "Neurometabolite", values_to = "Concentration")

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics_plot <- ggplot(DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics, 
                                                    aes(x = scandate, 
                                                        y = Concentration, 
                                                        color = Neurometabolite)) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.5), size = 4, alpha = 0.4) +
  xlab("Scan date") + ylab("Absolute concentrations, mM") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 16)) +
  theme_classic() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size=16, face="bold"),
                          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),
                          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),  
                          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),
                          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
                          strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          strip.background = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Parietal white matter") 
```

![](C:/Users/fbert/Dropbox/LABORAL/UCT/Research Article 2/Exploratory analysis plots/MRS data diagnostics/Absolute mM PGM.png){width="100%"}

![](C:/Users/fbert/Dropbox/LABORAL/UCT/Research Article 2/Exploratory analysis plots/MRS data diagnostics/Absolute mM PWM.png){width="100%"}

<br>

#### **Potential cause of these irregularities**

Upon investigation, we determined that this discrepancy resulted from an undocumented alteration in the positioning of the MRS voxel used for water referencing in the white matter sequence. Puzzlingly, this post-lockdown modification was not mirrored in the grey matter acquisition. The degree of misalignment between the water reference voxel and the metabolite voxel directly influenced the severity of the observed deviations, leading to unreliable absolute concentration estimates for white matter metabolites acquired post-lockdown.

<br>

#### **Decisions on data analysis**

Given the methodological inconsistency identified, we implemented the following data selection strategy to ensure scientific rigor and validity:

- **Neurometabolite ratios** to total creatine: All available data (pre- and post-lockdown) were retained for both parietal grey and white matter analyses, as these measures remained within physiologically plausible ranges and were not directly affected by the misalignment issue.

- **Absolute concentrations in the parietal grey matter** voxel: All available data (pre- and post-lockdown) were used, as the water reference voxel placement was consistent throughout the study period. Identified outliers, belonging to first scans post-lockdown, were removed using the IQR method.

- **Absolute concentrations in the parietal white matter** voxel: There are two potential approaches, including (a) a *conservative approach* where only pre-lockdown data were utilized, given the unreliability of post-lockdown absolute concentrations due to voxel misalignment; and (b) a *less conservative approach* where post-lockdown data within the range of pre-lockdown data (i.e., with no voxel misalignment) will also be utilized

<br>

#### **Potential bias and limitations**

The exclusion of post-lockdown absolute metabolite concentration data in our parietal white matter dataset introduces potential selection bias, as only a subset of participants will contribute to this specific analysis. This restriction will reduce statistical power and may alter cohort representativeness if sociodemographic or clinical differences exist between pre- and post-lockdown participants. To mitigate bias, we will conduct comparative analyses between pre- and post-lockdown subgroups using available demographic and clinical covariates to assess potential systematic differences. Where necessary, statistical adjustments may be applied to ensure robustness in analyses interpreting white matter metabolite concentrations.

<br>
<br>
<br>

### **Data normality**

<br>

**Absolute metabolite concentrations**

To identify and exclude extreme outliers in neurometabolite ratios, we applied the **IQR method**. For each variable, values falling below Q1 - 3 × IQR or above Q3 + 3 × IQR were considered extreme outliers and flagged for exclusion. 

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm

identify_outliers <- function(data, variables) {
  outlier_list <- list()
  
  for (var in variables) {
    Q1 <- quantile(data[[var]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
    Q3 <- quantile(data[[var]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
    IQR_value <- Q3 - Q1
    
    lower_bound <- Q1 - 3 * IQR_value
    upper_bound <- Q3 + 3 * IQR_value
    
    outliers <- data %>%
      filter(data[[var]] < lower_bound | data[[var]] > upper_bound) %>%
      select(all_of(var))
    
    outlier_list[[var]] <- outliers
  }
  
  return(outlier_list)
}

abs_list <- c("Glu", "Ins", "NAA", "GPC+PCh", "Cr+PCr")
abs_list_outliers <- identify_outliers(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs, abs_list)

abs_list_outliers
```

<br>

These outliers are subsequently excluded from the analysis.

```{r}
remove_outliers <- function(data, variables) {
  for (var in variables) {
    Q1 <- quantile(data[[var]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
    Q3 <- quantile(data[[var]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
    IQR_value <- Q3 - Q1

    lower_bound <- Q1 - 3 * IQR_value
    upper_bound <- Q3 + 3 * IQR_value

    data <- data %>%
      mutate(!!sym(var) := ifelse(.data[[var]] < lower_bound | .data[[var]] > upper_bound, NA, .data[[var]]))
  }
  return(data)
}

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs <- remove_outliers(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs, abs_list)
```

<br>

**Absolute metabolite concentrations**

As per previously described issues on this dataset, we are only analysing a subset of these scans. There are two approaches:

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm 

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_pre <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs %>%
  filter(scandate < ymd("2020-03-01"))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_post <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs %>%
  filter(scandate >= ymd("2020-03-01"))


preCOVID_thresholds <- function(data, variables) {
  thresholds <- list()
  
  for (var in variables) {
    Q1 <- quantile(data[[var]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
    Q3 <- quantile(data[[var]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
    IQR_value <- Q3 - Q1
    
    lower_bound <- Q1 - 3 * IQR_value
    upper_bound <- Q3 + 3 * IQR_value
    
    thresholds[[var]] <- list(lower = lower_bound, upper = upper_bound)
  }
  
  return(thresholds)
}

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_pre_outlier_thresholds <- preCOVID_thresholds(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_pre, 
                                                                             abs_list)

postCOVID_filter <- function(data, thresholds, variables) {
  for (var in variables) {
    lower_bound <- thresholds[[var]]$lower
    upper_bound <- thresholds[[var]]$upper
    
    data <- data %>%
      filter(.data[[var]] >= lower_bound & .data[[var]] <= upper_bound)
  }
  return(data)
}

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_post_filtered <- postCOVID_filter(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_post, 
                                                                 DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_pre_outlier_thresholds, 
                                                                 abs_list)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_flexible <- bind_rows(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_pre, 
                                                     DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_post_filtered)


```

<br>

If we only use pre-lockdown data (conservative approach), our subset is reduced to n=67 scans (10 CHEU, 57 CHU)

```{r}
summary(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_pre$maternal_hiv)

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics_pre <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_pre %>%
  select(scandate, Glu, Ins, NAA, `GPC+PCh`, maternal_hiv, sex) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(scandate, maternal_hiv, sex), names_to = "Neurometabolite", values_to = "mM")

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics_pre_plot <- ggplot(DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics_pre, 
                                                    aes(x = scandate, 
                                                        y = mM, 
                                                        color = Neurometabolite)) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.5), size = 4, alpha = 0.4) +
  xlab("Scan date") + ylab("Absolute concentrations, mM") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 16)) +
  theme_classic() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size=16, face="bold"),
                          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),
                          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, colour = "black"),  
                          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),
                          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
                          strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
                          strip.background = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Parietal white matter, conservative") 

DCHS_MRI_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_diagnostics_pre_plot
```

### **Simple analysis of neurometabolite group differences** 
[Go back to index](#top)

#### **Parietal grey matter**
[Go back to index](#top)

Summary statistics

```{r}
ratios_list <- c("Glu/Cr+PCr", "Ins/Cr+PCr", "NAA/Cr+PCr", "GPC+PCh/Cr+PCr")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_reshaped <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios %>%
  gather(Neurometabolite, Levels, all_of(ratios_list))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_summary_table <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_reshaped %>%
  group_by(maternal_hiv, Neurometabolite) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(Levels)), 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_summary_table_wide <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_summary_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = maternal_hiv, values_from = c("Mean", "SD", "Median", "IQR", "n"))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_reshaped <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs %>%
  gather(Neurometabolite, Levels, all_of(abs_list))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_summary_table <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_reshaped %>%
  group_by(maternal_hiv, Neurometabolite) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(Levels)), 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_summary_table_wide <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_summary_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = maternal_hiv, values_from = c("Mean", "SD", "Median", "IQR", "n"))
```

<br>

Children who are HEU were found to have lower glutamate ratios to creatine (`Glu/Cr+PCr`) in the midline parietal grey matter at age 6-7 years. Group data is expressed as raw median (IQR); *t*-tests were performed on scaled data.

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_test_analysis <- ratios_list %>%
  map_df(function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste("`", x, "` ~ maternal_hiv", sep = ""))
    
    t_test_result <- t.test(formula = f, data = DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_scaled)
    
    tibble(
      Neurometabolite = x,
      ttest_estimate = t_test_result$estimate,
      ttest_conf_low = t_test_result$conf.int[1],
      ttest_conf_high = t_test_result$conf.int[2],
      ttest_p = t_test_result$p.value
    )
  }) %>%
  distinct(Neurometabolite, .keep_all = TRUE)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_summary_table_wide_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_summary_table_wide %>%
  filter(Neurometabolite %in% ratios_list)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_summary_table_wide_test,
                                                     DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_test_analysis, 
                                                     by = "Neurometabolite")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(
    n1 = `n_HIV negative`,  
    n2 = `n_HIV positive`  
  ) %>%
  select(-`n_HIV negative`, -`n_HIV positive`)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(ttest_p), ~ round(.x, 2)))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$CHU <- paste(
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$`Median_HIV negative`,
  "(", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$`IQR_HIV negative`, ") (n=",
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$n1, ")"
)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$CHEU <- paste(
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$`Median_HIV positive`,
  "(", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$`IQR_HIV positive`, ") (n=",
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$n2, ")"
)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test %>%
  select(Neurometabolite, CHU, CHEU, ttest_estimate, ttest_conf_low, ttest_conf_high, ttest_p)

# Rename 
names(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test) <- c("Ratios", "CHU", "CHEU", "Mean Difference", 
                                                       "T-test 95% CI (low)", "T-test 95% CI (high)", "T-test P value")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test_kable <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test, "html") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test$`T-test P value` < 0.05)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ALL_test_kable
```

<br>

Analyses revealed no group differences in neurometabolite absolute concentrations between children who are HEU and HU in the midline parietal grey matter. Group data is expressed as raw median (IQR); *t*-tests were performed on scaled data.

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_test_analysis <- abs_list %>%
  map_df(function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste("`", x, "` ~ maternal_hiv", sep = ""))
    
    t_test_result <- t.test(formula = f, data = DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_scaled)
    
    tibble(
      Neurometabolite = x,
      ttest_estimate = t_test_result$estimate,
      ttest_conf_low = t_test_result$conf.int[1],
      ttest_conf_high = t_test_result$conf.int[2],
      ttest_p = t_test_result$p.value
    )
  }) %>%
  distinct(Neurometabolite, .keep_all = TRUE)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_summary_table_wide_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_summary_table_wide %>%
  filter(Neurometabolite %in% abs_list)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_summary_table_wide_test,
                                                     DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_test_analysis, 
                                                     by = "Neurometabolite")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(
    n1 = `n_HIV negative`,  
    n2 = `n_HIV positive`  
  ) %>%
  select(-`n_HIV negative`, -`n_HIV positive`)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(ttest_p), ~ round(.x, 2)))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test$CHU <- paste(
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test$`Median_HIV negative`,
  "(", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test$`IQR_HIV negative`, ") (n=",
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test$n1, ")"
)

  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test$n2, ")"
)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test %>%
  select(Neurometabolite, CHU, CHEU, ttest_estimate, ttest_conf_low, ttest_conf_high, ttest_p)

# Rename 
names(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test) <- c("Abs (mM)", "CHU", "CHEU", "Mean Difference", 
                                                       "T-test 95% CI (low)", "T-test 95% CI (high)", "T-test P value")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test_kable <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ALL_test, "html") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
```

```{r}
# Parietal grey matter

  # Ratios to creatine
  
    DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_reshaped$Neurometabolite <- factor(
      DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_reshaped$Neurometabolite, 
      levels = c("Glu/Cr+PCr",
                 "Ins/Cr+PCr",
                 "NAA/Cr+PCr",
                 "GPC+PCh/Cr+PCr"),
      labels = c("Glutamate",
                 "Myo-inositol",
                 "N-acetyl-aspartate",
                 "Total choline"))
    
    DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ggbopxplot <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_reshaped %>%
      ggplot(aes(y = Levels, x = maternal_hiv, color = maternal_hiv, fill = maternal_hiv)) +
      facet_wrap(~Neurometabolite, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
      geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.25), size = 2, alpha = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
      geom_boxplot(lwd = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", alpha = 0.5, width = .1, show.legend = FALSE) +
      ylab("Ratios to creatine") + xlab("") + scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CHU", "CHEU")) +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.background = element_blank()
      ) +
      ggtitle("Midline parietal grey matter") +
      scale_color_manual(values = specific_colors)
    
    # DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ggbopxplot
    
    ggsave("C:/Users/fbert/Dropbox/LABORAL/UCT/Research Article 2/Exploratory analysis plots/Neurometabolite group comparisons/PGM_ratios.png", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_ratios_ggbopxplot, width = 4, height = 4, dpi = 1200)
    
  # Absolute concentrations

    DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_reshaped$Neurometabolite <- factor(
      DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_reshaped$Neurometabolite, 
      levels = c("Glu",
                 "Ins",
                 "NAA",
                 "GPC+PCh",
                 "Cr+PCr"),
      labels = c("Glutamate",
                 "Myo-inositol",
                 "N-acetyl-aspartate",
                 "Total choline",
                 "Total creatine"))
    
    DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ggbopxplot <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_reshaped %>%
      ggplot(aes(y = Levels, x = maternal_hiv, color = maternal_hiv, fill = maternal_hiv)) +
      facet_wrap(~Neurometabolite, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
      geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.25), size = 2, alpha = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
      geom_boxplot(lwd = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", alpha = 0.5, width = .1, show.legend = FALSE) +
      ylab("Absolute concentrations (mM)") + xlab("") + scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CHU", "CHEU")) +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.background = element_blank()
      ) +
      ggtitle("Midline parietal grey matter") +
      scale_color_manual(values = specific_colors)
    
    # DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ggbopxplot
    
    ggsave("C:/Users/fbert/Dropbox/LABORAL/UCT/Research Article 2/Exploratory analysis plots/Neurometabolite group comparisons/PGM_abs.png", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pgm_abs_ggbopxplot, width = 4, height = 6, dpi = 1200)
```

#### **Parietal white matter**

Summary statistics

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_reshaped <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios %>%
  gather(Neurometabolite, Levels, all_of(ratios_list))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_summary_table <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_reshaped %>%
  group_by(maternal_hiv, Neurometabolite) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(Levels)), 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_summary_table_wide <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_summary_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = maternal_hiv, values_from = c("Mean", "SD", "Median", "IQR", "n"))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_reshaped <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_flexible %>%
  gather(Neurometabolite, Levels, all_of(abs_list))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_summary_table <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_reshaped %>%
  group_by(maternal_hiv, Neurometabolite) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(Levels, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(Levels)), 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_summary_table_wide <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_summary_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = maternal_hiv, values_from = c("Mean", "SD", "Median", "IQR", "n"))
```

<br>

Children who are HEU were found to have lower choline ratios to creatine (`GPC+PCh/Cr+PCr`) in the left parietal white matter at age 6-7 years. Group data is expressed as raw median (IQR); *t*-tests were performed on scaled data.

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_test_analysis <- ratios_list %>%
  map_df(function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste("`", x, "` ~ maternal_hiv", sep = ""))
    
    t_test_result <- t.test(formula = f, data = DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_scaled)
    
    tibble(
      Neurometabolite = x,
      ttest_estimate = t_test_result$estimate,
      ttest_conf_low = t_test_result$conf.int[1],
      ttest_conf_high = t_test_result$conf.int[2],
      ttest_p = t_test_result$p.value
    )
  }) %>%
  distinct(Neurometabolite, .keep_all = TRUE)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_summary_table_wide_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_summary_table_wide %>%
  filter(Neurometabolite %in% ratios_list)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_summary_table_wide_test,
                                                     DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_test_analysis, 
                                                     by = "Neurometabolite")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(
    n1 = `n_HIV negative`,  
    n2 = `n_HIV positive`  
  ) %>%
  select(-`n_HIV negative`, -`n_HIV positive`)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(ttest_p), ~ round(.x, 2)))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$CHU <- paste(
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$`Median_HIV negative`,
  "(", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$`IQR_HIV negative`, ") (n=",
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$n1, ")"
)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$CHEU <- paste(
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$`Median_HIV positive`,
  "(", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$`IQR_HIV positive`, ") (n=",
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$n2, ")"
)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test %>%
  select(Neurometabolite, CHU, CHEU, ttest_estimate, ttest_conf_low, ttest_conf_high, ttest_p)

# Rename 
names(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test) <- c("Ratios", "CHU", "CHEU", "Mean Difference", 
                                                       "T-test 95% CI (low)", "T-test 95% CI (high)", "T-test P value")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test_kable <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test, "html") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test$`T-test P value` < 0.05)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_ratios_ALL_test_kable
```

<br>

Analyses revealed no group differences in neurometabolite absolute concentrations between children who are HEU and HU in the left parietal white matter. Group data is expressed as raw median (IQR); *t*-tests were performed on scaled data. 

Given the small sample size we are underpowered to detect differences, but under close observation, **myo-inositol mM** showed a trend towards being higher in HEU vs HU. 

```{r}
DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_test_analysis <- abs_list %>%
  map_df(function(x) {
    f <- as.formula(paste("`", x, "` ~ maternal_hiv", sep = ""))
    
    t_test_result <- t.test(formula = f, data = DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_flexible_scaled)
    
    tibble(
      Neurometabolite = x,
      ttest_estimate = t_test_result$estimate,
      ttest_conf_low = t_test_result$conf.int[1],
      ttest_conf_high = t_test_result$conf.int[2],
      ttest_p = t_test_result$p.value
    )
  }) %>%
  distinct(Neurometabolite, .keep_all = TRUE)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_summary_table_wide_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_summary_table_wide %>%
  filter(Neurometabolite %in% abs_list)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test <- left_join(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_summary_table_wide_test,
                                                     DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_test_analysis, 
                                                     by = "Neurometabolite")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(
    n1 = `n_HIV negative`,  
    n2 = `n_HIV positive`  
  ) %>%
  select(-`n_HIV negative`, -`n_HIV positive`)  

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(ttest_p), ~ round(.x, 2)))

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$CHU <- paste(
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$`Median_HIV negative`,
  "(", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$`IQR_HIV negative`, ") (n=",
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$n1, ")"
)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$CHEU <- paste(
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$`Median_HIV positive`,
  "(", DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$`IQR_HIV positive`, ") (n=",
  DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$n2, ")"
)

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test <- DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test %>%
  select(Neurometabolite, CHU, CHEU, ttest_estimate, ttest_conf_low, ttest_conf_high, ttest_p)

# Rename 
names(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test) <- c("Abs (mM)", "CHU", "CHEU", "Mean Difference", 
                                                       "T-test 95% CI (low)", "T-test 95% CI (high)", "T-test P value")

DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test_kable <- kable(DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test, "html") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(column = 7, bold = DCHS_biomarker_MRS_6yr_pwm_abs_ALL_test$`T-test P value` < 0.05)
```
